rulings.Case Title: KIERAN BUCKLEY et al VS. DAVID BARTENWERFER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Order Establishing Admissions And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 8, 2011, Line 1. PLAINTIFFS KIERAN BUCKLEY AND VERONICA CAMPBELL'S Notice Of Motion For Order Establishing Admissions And For Sanctions. Denied. The moving party failed to provide courtesy copies of the moving papers in compliance with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KONSTANTIN KUPFER VS. FARMERS INSURANCE GROUP, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 8, 2011, Line 4. DEFENDANT MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Granted. Defendant satisfied its initial burden of production and plaintiff did not present any evidence creating a triable issue that defendant breached the parties¿ insurance contract or the covenant of good faith therein. The undisputed facts establish that there was a genuine dispute regarding the amount of plaintiff¿s claim thereby precluding liability for insurance bad faith. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: TYRONE L. ADAMS VS. CHARLES L. EASLEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 8, 2011, Line 10. DEFENDANT CHARLEEN GOODRICH, JACK BASS, ANN FORD, GEORGE TREFCER, HOMEPOINTE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, ROBERT MACHADO, EILLEEN STEARMAN DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained as to all causes of action that are the subject of the demurrer. As to the demurring defendants who are named as defendants in the Sutter County action and have not been dismissed from that action, this case is abated until the conclusion of the Sutter County action or such earlier time when any of the defendants are dismissed from that action. As to the demurring defendants who are not named in the Sutter County action or have been dismissed from that action, plaintiff is granted leave to amend his claims but must do so in a complaint that is no longer than twenty pages and is not repetitious or filled with legalistic language. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD HYLEN VS. WILLIAM MITCHELL RYDER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Richard Hylen'S Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 8, 2011, Line 14. Plaintiff Richard Hylen'S Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction. Both parties are entitled to receive a gross payment of $7240.70 every four weeks less credit card charges attributable to them. If the parties cannot agree on whether this amount is employee compensation, a distribution of or a draw against profits, withdrawal of capital or something else, subject to a final characterization at trial, the amounts will be treated as a draw against profits. Hylen is entitled to recover past amounts since the date Ryder listed himself as an employee with Paychex. If, however, Ryder does not return to and remain at work full-time (excepting vacations and short illnesses) by January 1, 2012 the payments cease as of January 1, 2012 or such later date that Ryder ceases to work full-time. Hylen has full authority to choose Satel¿s payroll provider and is Satel¿s representative for that provider. The court finds that the parties had an agreement to pay themselves while each were working full-time for Satel and Hylen unilaterally and without authority breached that agreement to the detriment of Ryder. The court further finds that, due to Ryder¿s recent leave of absence and his listing himself as an employee with Paychex without disclosing this to Hylen, Hylen should be assigned the responsibility to work with Satel¿s payroll provider. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
